column2: vēnērunt Gentēs in hērēditātem tuam~; pollūvērunt templum sanctum tuum~; posuērunt Jerūsalim in pōmōrum custōdiam. ^ O God
column3: the heathens are come into thy inheritance~; they have defiled thy holy temple~; they have set Jerusalem as a safekeep of fruits.

column1: 2 ^ Posuērunt morticīna servōrum tuōrum ēscās vulātilibus caelī~; carnēs sanctōrum tuōrum bēstiīs terrae. ^ They have given the dead bodies of thy servants to be meat for the fowls of the air~; the flesh of thy saints for the beasts of the earth.
3 ^ Effūdērunt sanguinem eōrum tamquam aquam in circumitū Jerūsalīme
column2: et non erat quī sepelīret. ^ They have poured out their blood as water round about Jerusalem
column3: and there was none to bury them.

column1: 4 ^ Factī sumus opprobrium vīcīnīs nostrīs~; subsannātiō et illūsiō hīs quī in circumitū nostrō sunt. ^ We are become a reproach to our neighbours~; a scorn and derision to them that are round about us.

column2: Domine~? ¿~Īrāsceris in fīnem~? ¿~Accendētur velut ignis zēlus tuus~? ^ How long
column3: O Lord~? Wilt thou be angry for ever~? Shall thy zeal be kindled like a fire~?

column1: 6 ^ Effunde īram tuam in Gentēs quae tē non nōvērunt
column2: et in regna quae nōmen tuum non invocāvērunt
column3: ^ Pour out thy wrath upon the nations that have not known thee
column4: and upon the kingdoms that have not called upon thy name

column2: et locum ejus dēsōlāvērunt. ^ because they have devoured Jacob~; and have laid waste his place.

column1: 8 ^ Nē meminerīs inīquitātum nostrārum antīquārum~; cito anticipent nōs misericordiae tuae
column2: quia pauperēs factī sumus nimis. ^ Remember not our former iniquities~; let thy mercies speedily prevent us
column3: for we are become exceeding poor.

column10: and for the glory of thy name
column13: and forgive us our sins for thy name's sake

column3: et innōtēscat in nātiōnibus cōram oculīs nostrīs ultiō sanguinis servōrum tuōrum quī effūsus est. ^ lest they should say among the Gentiles
column5: and let the revenging blood of thy servants
column7: be made known among the nations before our eyes.

column1: 11 ^ Introiat in conspectū tuō gemitus compedītōrum~; secundum magnitūdinem bracchiī tuī possidē fīliōs mortificātōrum
column2: ^ Let the sighing of the prisoners come in before thee. According to the greatness of thy arm
column3: take possession of the children of them that have been put to death.

column1: 12 ^ et redde vīcīnīs nostrīs septuplum in sinū eōrum
column3: Domine. ^ And render to our neighbours sevenfold in their bosom
column4: the reproach of the same wherewith they have reproached thee

column3: confitēbimur tibī in saeculum~; in generātiōnem et generātiōnem adnuntiābimus laudem tuam. ^ But we thy people
column4: and the sheep of thy pasture
column5: will give thanks to thee for ever. We will show forth thy praise
